moat.description: A global duopoly with Moody's in debt ratings. Regulatory and brand moat.
moat.analysisSummary: S&P Global operates a **Financial Toll Bridge**:

analysisPoints.title: Regulatory Oligopoly
analysisPoints.text: You cannot issue global debt without a rating from S&P or Moody's. It is a legally-embedded requirement for institutional investors.

analysisPoints.title: IP Moat
analysisPoints.text: The S&P 500 brand is the most licensed index in the world. Asset managers pay SPGI every time a new ETF is created.

analysisPoints.title: Low Capex
analysisPoints.text: Once the rating methodologies and data platforms are built, every additional dollar of revenue flows straight to the bottom line.

growth.description: FY2025 revenue grew ~9% YoY driven by a strong refinancing wave lifting Ratings, continued ETF AUM expansion fuelling Index licensing, and steady Market Intelligence subscription growth. FY2026 guidance of 7–9% reflects some caution around tariff-driven debt issuance freezes, partially offset by a $10T+ corporate refinancing wall providing a durable multi-year ratings tailwind. Private credit ratings are emerging as a new, incremental revenue stream as the $1.5T+ market matures.

growthAnalysis.scoreDerivation: Base: 75 (stable financial data oligopoly with secular tailwinds) +5 (refinancing wall + ETF AUM expansion; private credit ratings emerging as incremental TAM) -2 (tariff uncertainty suppressing near-term issuance volumes; ESG data political headwinds in US) = 78
growthAnalysis.keyRisk: Tariff-driven economic slowdown freezes corporate debt issuance for 6–12 months, simultaneously reducing Ratings revenue and Market Intelligence terminal renewal rates below 95%

valuation.description: At ~$390 — just above the bear case ($380) — SPGI is at its most attractive entry in several years. Tariff-driven issuance uncertainty has pushed the stock to near-bear pricing despite fundamentals remaining intact. The refinancing wall, index licensing growth, and private credit expansion are multi-year tailwinds that the market is temporarily discounting. Forward P/E near 22× is a material discount to the 5-year average of ~30×.

bear.description: Tariff-driven economic slowdown severely curtails debt issuance for 12+ months, ESG backlash spreads internationally, and multiple compresses toward 18× earnings.
points: Tariff escalation triggers a corporate debt issuance freeze lasting 12+ months, cutting Ratings revenue 15–20%
ESG/sustainability data backlash spreads from US to European institutional clients, reducing data segment growth
Financial sector consolidation and AI-powered alternatives reduce Market Intelligence terminal renewal rates below 93%

base.description: The $10T+ refinancing wall drives sustained ratings demand through 2027, index licensing compounds on ETF AUM growth, and private credit ratings emerge as a new revenue stream.
points: Corporate refinancing wall sustains 7–9% ratings revenue growth through 2027 despite near-term issuance volatility
Index licensing grows 12–15% annually as global ETF AUM continues compounding; S&P 500 remains the most licensed benchmark
Private credit ratings adopted as standard by major direct lenders, adding $200–300M incremental annual revenue by 2027

bull.description: Private market data becomes a category-defining $1B+ business and AI-powered market intelligence commands premium pricing, driving a significant earnings re-rating.
points: Private credit and private equity data analytics compound to $1B+ revenue, valued at a premium SaaS multiple
AI-enhanced Market Intelligence platform enables meaningful price increases across the enterprise subscriber base
FCF generation exceeds $5B annually, supporting 15%+ dividend growth and continued buybacks that accelerate EPS compounding

learnedInterfaces.note: Financial data terminal interfaces are being streamlined and commoditized by AI-powered analytics platforms.

businessLogic.note: Credit rating methodologies are proprietary, regulatory-recognized, and legally required — AI enhances but cannot replace the NRSRO designation.

publicDataAccess.note: Some financial market data is becoming more accessible through open-source and alternative data providers.

talentScarcity.note: SEC-recognized credit analysts, regulatory relations specialists, and 160-year institutional knowledge cannot be replicated.

bundling.note: Credit Ratings + Market Intelligence + Platts Commodity Data + Mobility Data = a comprehensive financial intelligence bundle.

proprietaryData.note: 160 years of credit ratings history, Platts energy commodity benchmarks, and proprietary financial data — legally embedded in markets.

regulatoryLockIn.note: SEC-recognized NRSRO status is a legal moat. Replicating this designation requires decades of track record and regulatory approval.

networkEffects.note: Credit ratings are network-critical — bond issuers MUST use NRSRO-recognized agencies; investors MUST reference them.

transactionEmbedding.note: S&P ratings are legally embedded in every major bond covenant, loan agreement, regulatory filing, and pension fund mandate.

systemOfRecord.note: The authoritative system of record for global credit risk — no alternative source carries the same legal and institutional weight.

tenMoats.verdict: S&P Global's regulatory moat (NRSRO status) and role as the definitive system of record for credit risk makes them uniquely AI-resilient. AI disrupts analysis, not the legal requirement to use S&P ratings.
